place: Dryden village, New York, New York

facts.text: Dryden was named by surveyor Robert Harpur in 1790 after the English poet John Dryden, whose translations of Virgil inspired nearby town names.
Dryden Village was incorporated on July 7, 1857, and still stands as the town’s largest community, noted for its historic homes.
Southworth Library, designed by Ithaca architect William Henry Miller in Romanesque Revival style and completed in 1894, was funded by Jennie McGraw Fiske, who also donated Cornell’s chimes.
Three barns on a historic farmstead in Dryden contain 2.5 miles of shelving lined with thousands of books.
The village’s historic house is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
New York State Route 13 intersects New York State Route 38 at Dryden village.
